name: Beef Ragout w/ Sun-Dried Tomatoes & Winter Sq
recipeIngredient: 4 lb Beef chuck, trimmed and cut into cubes
4 cups Butternut squash, peeled and cut into 1-inch chunks
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring a small saucepan of water to a boil. Add the tomatoes; cover and remove from heat. Let them stand for 20 minutes to soften, then drain and quarter. Set aside.
2. In a large plastic or paper bag, coat half of the beef with flour, shaking off excess. Place in a single layer on wax paper.
3. Warm three tablespoons of olive oil in an 8-quart Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add beef in batches so the pan is not crowded. Cook until browned on all sides. If needed, add additional oil. Remove to a large plate.
4. Warm remaining one tablespoon of oil in the same pan over medium high heat. Add onion; cook for 10 minutes, or until lightly browned, stirring occasionally. Reduce heat, then add carrots and cook for 3 minutes.
5. Add broth, wine, and vinegar, stirring to scrape up brown bits. Stir in quartered tomatoes, beef and juices, tomato paste, garlic, parsley, and thyme. Cover; bring to a boil.
6. Reduce heat and simmer for 1 1/2 hours, or until the meat is almost tender, stirring occasionally.
7. Add squash, salt, and pepper and bring back to a simmer. Cover and cook for 15 minutes, or until squash is fork-tender.
Dinner Plan:
Remove half of the stew to a microwave-safe container; cool, then freeze for later use. To the remaining stew, add peas (if desired) and serve warm. Approximate microwave times: Defrost 40 minutes; to reheat, add peas and cook for 8 minutes, stirring once or twice.
